title: ramona dorrii
intro: Ramona dorrii, also known as the mountain balm, is a perennial herb native to the western United States and Canada, characterized by its small white flowers and distinctive minty aroma. The medicinal properties of Ramona dorrii include its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The plant is rich in rosmarinic acid, which provides numerous health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Ramona dorrii can be prepared as a tea, salve, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote wound healing, alleviate cold and flu symptoms, and soothe digestive issues.
